patch.value:
# Spike — minimal VCS-style changeset workflow as a pm package

Build a small, throwaway-quality but end-to-end **exemplar package** that models a version-control-like domain purely on public pm SDK primitives: a `Changeset` custom type with a domain status graph (draft → proposed → merged/abandoned), `ref`/`branch` modeled as custom fields or link relations, domain verbs (`propose`, `merge`, `abandon`) as extension-registered commands, merge rules enforced by hooks, and domain state derived by replaying the audited history stream.

## Why
This is the acceptance vehicle for the beyond-PM platform story (parent epic pm-usfg): the vision claims one could build "a VCS like Git or a company's business logic" on the SDK. A spike either demonstrates that with today's primitives or produces a precise, itemized gap list.

## Deliverables
- Exemplar package (sandbox or packages/ exemplar, mirroring the pm-cbwg / pm-kanban archetype pattern) importing ONLY the public SDK (`src/sdk`), zero private core imports (boundary ratchet pm-8778 applies).
- A written gap report: every place the spike needed a workaround, filed as individual dedupe-checked SDK items under pm-usfg.
- Smoke test in a temp workspace proving create → propose → merge → history-replay round-trip.

## Acceptance criteria
Spike package activates cleanly; the changeset workflow round-trip passes in a sandbox smoke; gap report exists with each gap either filed as a pm item or explicitly waived; no private core imports.

## Sequencing
Depends on pm-cbwg (PM-domain exemplar first) and pm-3mna (schema/config primitives in the SDK).
Beyond-PM SDK exemplar spike: minimal VCS-style changeset workflow as a pm package (custom schema + event-sourced history + hooks)

Acceptance vehicle for story pm-8ngt: an SDK-only exemplar package modeling a Changeset domain (draft→proposed→merged/abandoned, domain verbs as extension commands, merge rules as hooks, state via history replay). Produces an itemized SDK gap report. Mirrors the pm-cbwg/pm-kanban archetype pattern.

Spike package activates cleanly importing only the public SDK (zero private core imports); create→propose→merge→history-replay round-trip passes a sandbox smoke; gap report exists with each gap filed as a pm item or explicitly waived

value.author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
value.text: Acceptance evidence 2026-07-18: @unbrained/pm-vcs ships 2 domain types, 4 fields, a custom commits_to ontology, VCS profile/workflow, 7 domain commands, reviewed-merge hook, point-in-time history reconstruction, and durable merge-ledger projection using public SDK contracts only. Linked tests pass 2/2. Packed manual proof installed the tarball into an isolated project, installed vcs, applied its profile, created/proposed/merged a changeset, reconstructed version 1, replayed version=1/processed=1 identically across fresh processes, passed isolated package doctor and tracker validation, and preserved normal core create/get.

value.author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
value.text: A foreign-domain package can remain core-independent when the host injects lifecycle, history, and relationship services. Runtime package imports stay type-only; values come from context.sdk, allowing bundled installed copies to execute without private paths or nested CLI resolution.

value.author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
value.text: Gap disposition: production content-addressed object storage, tree diffing, and network transport are VCS-product concerns, not universal SDK gaps. Cross-item plus relationship atomic commit remains an explicit bounded-exemplar waiver; larger durable indexing and scale work stays on canonical pm-ju83/pm-m2il lineage.

patch.value: Delivered and verified the public-SDK-only VCS changeset exemplar, gap report, durable replay, installed package lifecycle, and packed temp-workspace acceptance at exact full repository coverage.

value.author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
value.text: Pre-push Greptile corrections: VCS merge events now use stable merge-<changeset> identities and append without a stale read-then-write expectedVersion window, so concurrent independent merges serialize under the durable store lock. Installed SDK-dispatch acceptance proves packed-sdk-author reaches the immutable ledger; direct-source acceptance runs two independent merges concurrently.

value.author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
value.text: For durable domain commands, do not read currentVersion and then reacquire the append lock merely to supply that version: the gap creates avoidable conflicts across independent mutations. Omit the optional OCC precondition when the store lock itself defines serialization, and use a stable domain event id for duplicate detection.
